data.title: Elon Musk says Cybercab has entered production
data.description:
• “Cybercab has started production,” Elon Musk said in a post on X, sharing a video of the same.
  • Musk earlier this week highlighted new-product ramps while cautioning on pace: "We have just started production of Cybercab, and we'll begin production of our Semi truck soon."
  • While also saying initial output will be slow because "whenever you have a new product with a completely new supply chain... you should expect that initial production of Cybercab and Semi will be very slow, but then ramping up... towards the end of the year and certainly next year," he said on the conference call.

data.title: Asian markets retreat as stalled Iran peace talks fuel oil anxiety
ENI GAAP EPS of €0.04, revenue of €20.06B; reaffiems FY26 outlook and boosts 2026 share buyback to €2.8B
data.description:
• ENI press release (E): Q1 GAAP EPS of €0.04.
  • Revenue of €20.06B (+4.5% Y/Y).
  • Production 1.80M BOE/D (est 1.78M).
  • Adj. Oper Profit €2.42B (est €2.84B).
  • Boosts 2026 Share Buyback To €2.8B.
  • Confirms 2026 Outlook: Specifically our segmental guidance is providing: • FY’26 underlying oil&gas production growth expected at 3-4%. • FY’26 GGP adjusted proforma EBIT guided at around €1.3 bln, up 30% from the initial forecast. • Enilive and Plenitude: FY proforma adjusted EBITDA respectively of around €1.1 bln and €1.3 bln. • End of year installed renewable capacity at 6.5 GW (Plenitude @100%); biorefinery capacity at 2.1 MTPA plus 2 MTPA under construction (net Enilive). On the financial side, we expect: • At a revised Brent scenario of 83 $/bbl, SERM refining margin at 8 $/bbl, TTF gas price at 50 €/MWh, at an exchange rate EUR/USD of 1.15, adjusted CFFO to amount to €13.8 bln representing an underlying improvement vs Group’s sensitivities (€0.11 bln and €0.09 bln per each one-dollar change in the Brent price and SERM margin respectively; €0.03 bln for each one-euro per MWh change in the spot price of European gas). • Gross capex and net capex confirmed at €7 bln and €5 bln, respectively. • Gearing at the lower end of the 10-15% guided range. Shareholders’ returns: • Confirmed the planned 2026 dividend of €1.1 per share (up 5% vs. 2025).

data.title: SpaceX warns inquiries into sexually abusive AI imagery may hurt market access, Reuters reports
data.description:
The multiple investigations into xAI’s (X.AI) creation and dissemination of sexually abusive imagery may lead the company ​to lose access to certain markets, parent company SpaceX (SPACE) warned in a prospectus reviewed by Reuters.

In a section on risk factors, the S-1 regulatory ‌filing said a number of agencies around the world were “actively investigating and making inquiries relating to social media or the use of AI” in relation to advertising, consumer protection and the distribution of harmful content, among other matters.

The news comes after SpaceX hosted analysts at its Colossus supercomputer in Memphis, Tennessee, on Thursday, gearing up for its $1.75T IPO expected this summer. U.S. securities law ​requires companies to disclose such risk factors, alerting investors to potential pitfalls while also helping protect companies against future legal liability. The disclosures do not ​necessarily mean each listed outcome is expected to occur, the report added.

One challenge the Elon Musk-led company highlighted was that it faced “allegations that our AI products were used ⁠to create nonconsensual explicit images or content representing children in sexualized contexts," the S-1 document said. Such regulatory inquiries could expose SpaceX to lawsuits, liability and government ​action – “including loss of access to certain markets, which has occurred in the past,” the document stated.

The report said it was not ​clear whether potential regulatory action could prevent SpaceX as a whole from accessing certain markets or just its subsidiary, xAI, specifically.

data.title: Columbia Banking System expects net interest margin to cross 4% in Q2 while guiding $150M-$200M quarterly buybacks
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Columbia Banking System (COLB) Q1 2026

Management View

  • “Our first quarter results reflected continued execution against the same core priorities we have previously outlined, delivering consistent, repeatable results, optimizing our balance sheet and returning excess capital to shareholders.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Clint Stein)
  • “We also completed the Pac Premier systems conversion and consolidated 9 branches during the quarter, putting us on track for full realization of all acquisition-related cost savings by the end of this quarter.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Stein)
  • “Given our current capital position and strong forward outlook, we increased our pace of buybacks during the first quarter, returning $200 million to our shareholders.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Stein)
  • “We reported earnings per share of $0.66 and operating earnings per share of $0.72 for the first quarter.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Ivan Seda)
  • “Net interest margin was 3.96% for the first quarter, right at the top end of the range that I outlined in our last call.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “New loan origination volume of $1.2 billion was up 38% from the year ago quarter.” (Senior EVP & Co-President of Columbia Bank Torran Nixon)

Outlook

  • “Following the modest earning asset contraction during the first quarter, we expect the balance sheet size to remain relatively stable with commercial loan growth offset by contraction in the transactional portfolio.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “We continue to expect noninterest revenues in the low to mid-$80 million range for Q2.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “Excluding CDI amortization, we expect noninterest expense in the $335 million to $345 million range for the second quarter before declining in the third quarter as we realize all cost savings related to the transaction by June 30.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “I think we will be roughly at that 4% marker here in Q2 of this year, and then we will continue to step up from there and move upwards north of 4% into the second half of the year.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “We expect share repurchases to remain in the $150 million to $200 million range per quarter through our current authorization.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)

Financial Results

  • “Average earning assets were $60.8 billion during the first quarter, coming in at the midpoint of the range that I outlined in January.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “Noninterest income in the first quarter was $83 million on a GAAP basis and $81 million on an operating basis.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “Noninterest expense… was $369 million on an operating basis. Excluding intangible amortization of $41 million, the first quarter’s $328 million run rate was below our guided range.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “Provision expense was $28 million for the first quarter.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “Our CET1 and total risk-based capital ratios declined modestly to 11.5% and 13.3%, respectively.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)

Q&A

  • Jon Arfstrom, RBC Capital Markets: “Can you guys talk a little bit about the $1.2 billion plus in originations kind of where that’s coming from…”; Senior EVP & Co-President Nixon: “It’s not coming from one particular part of the company. It’s spread throughout the organization.”
  • Jon Arfstrom, RBC Capital Markets: “Can you give us a little more on what you’re thinking on the margin?”; CFO & Executive Vice President Seda: “We will be roughly at that 4% marker here in Q2 of this year.”
  • David Feaster, Raymond James: “How did that go relative to expectations? How is feedback from clients? Have you seen any attrition?”; President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Stein: “It went so smooth that I almost forgot that we did a conversion… because there was no drama… no customer disruption.”
  • David Feaster, Raymond James: “Curious if you could discuss how active that you’ve been on the hiring front…”; Co-President Christopher Merrywell: “We’re looking at expanding our wealth management operation… Southern California footprint… with trust folks, financial advisers, private bankers and folks that focus on health care as well.”
  • David Feaster, Raymond James: “Have you done any work around what that could mean for you all… especially given the treatment of MSRs?”; CFO & Executive Vice President Seda: “Our current back-of-the-napkin analysis… shows that we have a benefit of potentially up to the ballpark of 100 basis points of CET1.”
  • Jeff Rulis, D.A. Davidson: “I wanted to get a little more color on the nonaccrual adds and some of the net charge-offs there within the ag book”; Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Frank Namdar: “It is really centered in one customer relationship… This particular one was in the hop industry.”
  • Matthew Clark, Piper Sandler: “Just a few cleanup credit questions…”; Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Namdar: “The uptick was centered in one commercial real estate loan that is really in the process of being paid off.”
  • Matthew Clark, Piper Sandler: “Just help us understand maybe why you maintained it…”; CFO & Executive Vice President Seda: “There is reinvestment happening over the next 2 quarters.”
  • Christopher McGratty, KBW: “Do you stay in that range in Q3?”; CFO & Executive Vice President Seda: “We’ll come in below that in the second half of the year.”
  • Christopher McGratty, KBW: “Any help on the tax rate?”; CFO & Executive Vice President Seda: “I would use a 25% all-in effective tax rate as you model it out.”
  • David Chiaverini, Jefferies: “Do you have similar campaigns planned… to continue the deposit momentum?”; Co-President Merrywell: “We’ll relaunch… in June, and then we’ll have another one that goes into the fall as well.”
  • Sun Young Lee, TD Cowen: “So does that get us to about $605 million-ish NIM for this… quarter?”; CFO & Executive Vice President Seda: “No, I think you’re thinking about it correctly.”
  • Anthony Elian, JPMorgan: “Can you give us some color on what you expect for 2Q deposits…”; CFO & Executive Vice President Seda: “We kind of reach a low point kind of in the mid- to late April time frame and then return to a rebound through May and through June.”
  • Samuel Varga, UBS: “Do you need to see these payoffs moderate to start producing loan growth?”; Senior EVP & Co-President Nixon: “We’ve got some… nice C&I growth for this quarter, feel really good about the pipeline… into 2027.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was slightly positive and detail-oriented, with validation-seeking questions on margin trajectory, deposit seasonality, PacPremier integration, and credit specifics, including “This all looks good, but maybe loans and margin…” (Jon Arfstrom, RBC Capital Markets).
  • Management tone was positive and confident in execution, emphasizing conversion outcomes and forward margin expectations, including “the first quarter setting the low watermark for 2026.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • Compared with Q4 2025, management language shifted from forward-looking conversion readiness to completed execution, including “We remain on track for another seamless systems conversion this quarter” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Stein, Q4) versus “we… completed the Pac Premier systems conversion” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Stein, Q1).

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 management emphasized completed PacPremier conversion and branch consolidation, while Q4 emphasized anticipation of the “seamless systems conversion this quarter.” (President, CEO & Chairman of the Board Stein)
  • Margin discussion moved from Q4 guidance framing (“we expect net interest margin in the first quarter to land in a range from 3.90% to 3.95%” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda, Q4)) to Q1 execution and timing (“Net interest margin was 3.96%… top end of the range” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda, Q1) and “roughly at that 4% marker… in Q2” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda, Q1)).
  • Capital return cadence increased from Q4’s “repurchased 3.7 million common shares” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda, Q4) to Q1’s “repurchased 6.5 million common shares.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • Analyst focus remained concentrated on deposit pricing/competition, loan mix (transactional runoff vs. C&I), PacPremier integration, and credit migration, with Q1 adding more detailed regulatory-capital optionality questions tied to proposed rule-making. (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)

Risks and Concerns

  • “Relationship in the agricultural industry drove a modest increase in net charge-offs and nonperforming assets relative to the fourth quarter.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)
  • “It is really centered in one customer relationship… cost inputs being extremely high and margins extremely tight.” (Executive VP & Chief Credit Officer Namdar)
  • “The headwind that we deal with every year in Q1 is the seasonality… deposit outflows and a heavier reliance on the wholesale funding channels.” (CFO & Executive Vice President Seda)

Final Takeaway

Management positioned Q1 as an execution quarter: PacPremier’s systems conversion was described as unusually smooth, synergy capture was reiterated as on track for completion by the end of Q2, and the balance sheet strategy stayed centered on remixing out of lower-yield transactional loans while keeping total balances “relatively stable.” The company reiterated expectations for net interest margin to cross 4% during Q2 and outlined continued capital return through buybacks, while acknowledging seasonal deposit headwinds and a specific agriculture-related credit issue that management described as relationship-specific rather than systemic.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Italy’s Eni boosts share buyback program to €2.8B
data.description: • Italian energy group ​Eni (E) increased its share buyback by around ​90% to ​2.8 billion euros ($3.27B) on Friday, ⁠and improved ​its 2026 guidance ​on cash flow from operations.

AB Volvo (publ) GAAP EPS of SEK 4.09, revenue of SEK 110.8B
data.description:
• AB Volvo (publ) press release (VLVLY): Q1 GAAP EPS of SEK 4.09.

  • Operating cash flow in the Industrial Operations amounted to SEK 432 M (1,309).
  • Return on capital employed in the Industrial Operations amounted to 24.5% (31.8).

data.title: TSMC hits record high after Taiwan loosens fund investment limits; Taiex climbs 3.3
data.description:
• Shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) rose to a record high on Friday after regulators loosened limits on single-stock investments, enabling fund managers to increase exposure to the world’s largest contract chipmaker
  • Shares of the chipmaker gained as much as 5.1% and the benchmark Taiex Index rose as much as 3.3%, the most in Asia.
  • Local equity funds and actively managed exchange-traded funds can invest up to 25% of their funds into listed companies whose weight exceeds 10% of the Taiwan Stock Exchange starting from Friday, Taiwan’s financial supervisory commission said on Thursday.
  • Previously, investment funds could only invest up to 10% of their total net asset value in one company. 
  • The move, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co., could draw more than $6B of inflows, Bloomberg reported.

ETFs linked to Taiwan include (FLTW), (EWT).
